stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a real, assessable phenomenon; operates or endorses PA-specific reunification / alienating-behaviour expert-witness work
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA construct is operationalised against domestic-abuse survivors; advocates DV/trauma-informed alternatives and tighter regulation
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both alienating-behaviour and family-violence frames; AFCC-aligned, differential-diagnosis (Fidler/Bala/Saini Children Who Resist) approach

canadian_context_notes: Federal Divorce Act amended by Bill C-78 (in force 1 Mar 2021) requires courts to consider 'family violence' (s.16(3)(j))
No Canadian appellate equivalent to UK Re Y [2026] EWFC 38 restricting unregulated PA experts
NAWL May 2024 brief to House of Commons FEWO calls for ban on PA accusations: https://nawl.ca/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/NAWL-FEWO-brief-PA-EN.pdf
Quebec Rebatir la confiance report addressed PAS weaponisation in DV cases

us_therapists: Linda Gottlieb LMFT LCSW-R (TPFF takes Canadian referrals)
Joan B. Kelly PhD (trains Canadian AFCC assessors)
canada_lawyers: Pamela Cross (co-developer of Jaffe e-learning course)
Nicholas Bala (Queen's Law; co-author with Fidler & Saini)

practitioners.name: Dr Kathleen M. Reay, PhD, RCC
practitioners.practice: Founder and Clinical Director, Family Reflections Reunification Program Inc. (Nanaimo/Vancouver Island, BC)
practitioners.regulatory_body: BC Association of Clinical Counsellors (BCACC) — RCC
practitioners.regulatory_notes: RCC (not registered psychologist with CHCPBC). PhD from Capella University 2007.
practitioners.specialty: 4-day intensive reunification retreat for severely alienated children and rejected parents; high-conflict divorce, EMDR-trauma
practitioners.stance_source: Reay (2015), 'Family Reflections: A Promising Therapeutic Program' AJFT 43(2):197-207 https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/01926187.2015.1007769 ; 2007 PhD dissertation on PAS long-term effects
programs_or_publications: Family Reflections Reunification Program (pilot 2012, 22 children/12 families)
Reay (2015) AJFT outcome paper claiming 95% success at 12-month follow-up
Reay & Bernet (2020) PARQ-Gap study, Journal of Forensic Sciences
Cited in Williamson v. Williamson 2016 BCCA 87
practitioners.primary_contact: https://familyreflectionsprogram.com/ contact form; PO Box 41023, Nanaimo, BC V9T 6M7
practitioners.hook: Most-named Canadian PA reunification programme in BC custody judgments; direct Canadian parallel to US Family Bridges.
practitioners.notes: RCC (not psychologist) status is the Canadian regulatory analogue of the post-Re Y UK debate. PARQ-Gap collaboration with Bernet places her firmly in PASG international network.

Office of the Children's Lawyer Clinical Investigator panel (since 2011)
Conference presentations on PA / voice of the child
practitioners.primary_contact: joanna@torontofamilytherapy.com ; (416) 629-7410 ; 1810 Avenue Road, Suite 200, Toronto, ON M5M 3Z2

practitioners.name: Dr Lorri Yasenik, PhD, MSW, RSW, RPT-S, CPT-S
practitioners.practice: Co-owner and Clinical Director, Rocky Mountain Play Therapy Institute (Calgary); Yasenik & Associates; co-founder ICCFL

practitioners.specialty: Play therapy; parenting coordination; child voices in legal processes; high-conflict separation/divorce; expert witness (~58 court appearances)
practitioners.stance_source: Yasenik & Graham 'Out of the Mouths of Babes' model and 'Decision-Making in Child Inclusive Practice' (ICCFL); AFCC Alberta PWG on Parenting Coordination membership https://www.afccalberta.org/pwgparentingcoordination ; PhD (2013)
programs_or_publications: Rocky Mountain Play Therapy Institute
International Centre for Children and Family Law (with Jon Graham, Sydney AU)
2013 PhD dissertation on children's voices in family legal process
AFCC Alberta PWG on Parenting Coordination member
practitioners.hook: AFCC-aligned Alberta child-inclusive voice; closest Canadian analogue to Joan Kelly differential approach; opens Alberta parenting-coordinator network.
practitioners.notes: Middle-leaning recognition. ICCFL co-founder Jon Graham is Sydney-based - source brief 'Dr Jon Graham Toronto' is incorrect.

practitioners.name: Dr Peter G. Jaffe, PhD, C.Psych.
practitioners.practice: Professor, Faculty of Education, Western University; Director Emeritus CREVAWC; Director Emeritus London Family Court Clinic
practitioners.regulatory_body: College of Psychologists of Ontario (C.Psych. per Western bio)
practitioners.registration_number: not publicly displayed on Western profile
practitioners.specialty: Child custody and domestic violence; expert-witness practice in Family Court; 2,000+ custody assessments supervised over 40 years
practitioners.stance_source: Jaffe, Crooks & Poisson (2003) Juvenile and Family Court Journal 54(4):57-67; Jaffe, Lemon & Poisson Child Custody and Domestic Violence (Sage 2003); co-presenter on CREVAWC 23 Mar 2021 webinar 'The Misuse of Alienation in DV Cases in Family Court' https://www.gbvlearningnetwork.ca/webinars/recorded-webinars/2021/webinar-2021-1.html
programs_or_publications: CREVAWC (founding partner 1992; AD 2005-2021)
London Family Court Clinic (Director Emeritus)
Child Custody and Domestic Violence (Sage 2003)
Free e-learning course on PA/parent-child contact problems (with Pamela Cross)
2021 Misuse of Alienation webinar
practitioners.primary_contact: pjaffe@uwo.ca ; Faculty of Education, Western University, London ON
practitioners.hook: Most-senior Canadian psychologist on the DV/PA-misuse side; co-developer with Pamela Cross of the free Canadian e-learning course used by judges and lawyers nationwide.

practitioners.name: Professor Simon Lapierre, PhD
practitioners.practice: Full Professor, School of Social Work, University of Ottawa; founding member, Feminist Anti-Violence (FemAnVi) Research Collective
practitioners.regulatory_body: Academic (PhD University of Warwick, UK) - not a clinician
practitioners.regulatory_notes: Academic/research role; social-work licensure framework does not apply
practitioners.specialty: International research on PA-allegations in DV contexts; Quebec PA institutionalisation; mothers' experiences of PA accusations
practitioners.stance_source: Lapierre, Ladouceur, Frenette & Cote (2020) 'The legitimization and institutionalization of parental alienation in the Province of Quebec' JSWFL 42(1):30-44 https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/09649069.2019.1701922 ; Lapierre et al. (2024) JSWFL 'parental alienation allegations in DV contexts' https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09649069.2024.2414623
programs_or_publications: 2020 JSWFL Quebec institutionalisation paper (foundational Canadian critique)
2024 JSWFL impacts-on-mother-child relationships paper
FemAnVi Research Collective (founding member)
Co-author with Suzanne Zaccour on Quebec PA/DV literature
practitioners.hook: Lapierre's 2020 JSWFL paper is the most-cited Canadian-language scholarly critique of how Quebec institutionalised PAS - entry-point article for entire francophone-Canada critique camp.

practitioners.name: Professor Linda C. Neilson, PhD
practitioners.practice: Professor Emerita, Department of Sociology, UNB; Research Fellow, Muriel McQueen Fergusson Centre for Family Violence Research (MMFC)
practitioners.regulatory_body: Academic - socio-legal scholar; not a clinician
practitioners.regulatory_notes: Sociology/socio-legal academic; regulatory framework does not apply
practitioners.specialty: Empirical analysis of Canadian family-court PA cases; legal system responses to DV; coordinated family-violence court model
practitioners.stance_source: Neilson (2018) Parental Alienation Empirical Analysis: Child Best Interests or Parental Rights? (MMFC/FREDA) https://www.fredacentre.com/wp-content/uploads/Parental-Alienation-Linda-Neilson.pdf - analysed 357 Canadian family-court PA cases 2008-2018; finding ~75% of PA claims in DV-allegation cases were made by alleged perpetrator; mothers twice as likely as fathers to lose primary custody on a PA finding
programs_or_publications: 2018 MMFC/FREDA Parental Alienation Empirical Analysis (357 cases)
Bill C-78 expert brief (House of Commons Justice 2018)
Responding to Domestic Violence in Family Law, Civil Protection & Child Protection Cases (CanLII e-text 2nd edn 2020)
Co-presenter CREVAWC 2021 webinar on Misuse of Alienation
practitioners.hook: Neilson's 357-case empirical analysis is THE Canadian data-anchor for PA-as-DV-survivor-suppressor thesis - cited by every brief to Parliament and every NAWL/Luke's Place submission.

practitioners.name: Suzanne Zaccour, LLB, BCL, DPhil (Oxon)
practitioners.practice: Director of Legal Affairs, National Association of Women and the Law (NAWL); Barreau du Quebec / Law Society of Ontario
practitioners.regulatory_body: Barreau du Quebec; Law Society of Ontario (lawyer, not clinician)
practitioners.regulatory_notes: Lawyer-academic; included as the political vehicle that operationalises therapist-critique camp evidence (Lapierre, Neilson, Boyd/Sheehy)
practitioners.specialty: Doctrinal and empirical critique of PA accusations in family court; lead author NAWL 2024 FEWO brief
practitioners.stance_source: Zaccour (2020) 'Does Domestic Violence Disappear from Parental Alienation Cases? Five Lessons from Quebec' Canadian Journal of Family Law 33(2) https://commons.allard.ubc.ca/can-j-fam-l/vol33/iss2/8/ ; NAWL (2024) 'Banning Parental Alienation Accusations in Family Court' https://nawl.ca/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/NAWL-FEWO-brief-PA-EN.pdf
programs_or_publications: 2020 Canadian Journal of Family Law 'Five Lessons' paper
2024 NAWL FEWO brief calling for statutory ban on PA accusations
Law360 column 'Silencing the abused' (2024)
practitioners.hook: Zaccour drafted the most-aggressive Canadian policy ask on PA to date - calling for outright statutory ban; unavoidable for any Canadian press-cycle response on PA-as-policy.
practitioners.notes: Strictly lawyer-academic, not a clinician - included because her work is the political vehicle operationalising therapist-critique evidence.

practitioners.name: Professor Rachel Birnbaum, PhD, RSW, LLM
practitioners.practice: Distinguished University Professor Emerita, School of Social Work and Childhood & Youth Studies, King's University College at Western; Adjunct Professor McGill Social Work
practitioners.regulatory_notes: PhD, LLM, RSW. Academic role primary; clinical-practice scale not publicly confirmed post-emerita.
practitioners.specialty: Voice of the child in family law; long-term outcomes of alienation cases; law/social-work intersection; DOJ Canada commissioned research
practitioners.stance_source: Birnbaum & Bala (2010) 'Toward the Differentiation of High-Conflict Families' FCR 48(3):403-416 https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/j.1744-1617.2010.01319.x ; DOJ Canada-commissioned voice-of-the-child research (2022); Canadian Foundation for Legal Research grant with Bala (2022) on long-term PA outcomes
programs_or_publications: Birnbaum & Bala (2010) FCR differentiation paper
DOJ Canada voice-of-the-child report (2022)
Canadian Foundation for Legal Research grant (2022) on long-term PA outcomes
Bill C-223 brief to Parliament (joint with Bala et al.)
practitioners.hook: Birnbaum's longitudinal Bala-collaboration is the cleanest Canadian middle-ground evidence stream - useful as balanced-quote source.
practitioners.notes: Middle-leaning critique. Cross-reference /practitioners/lawyers/canada (already noted as academic). Source brief classification as 'middle' is defensible; classified critique here to reflect policy alignment with DV-attentive camp.

AFCC Overcoming Barriers HCDC staff psychologist

practitioners.notes: Middle but recognition-tilting - quoted by CTV (2019) supporting WHO recognition while operating differential-diagnosis clinical model.

honest_gaps: Quebec/francophone coverage thin - only Lapierre (Ottawa francophone) and Zaccour (QC-licensed lawyer-academic). Sophie Glasner-Mansour lead unverifiable against OPQ register. Dedicated Quebec batch would surface more OPQ practitioners.
Atlantic Canada coverage is one entry (Neilson, UNB).

Recognition camp thin (3 entries) - reflects Canadian regulatory reality. Most named recognition practitioners cluster around Reay's Family Reflections (BC), Toronto reunification (Seidel), or AFCC-aligned middle.
Source-brief verification rate low for unverified names (1 of 7 verified: Yasenik only). Glazer, Black, Davidson, Hicks, Knapp-Hermer, Thompson, Glasner-Mansour all unverifiable.
CPO/OCSWSSW registration numbers not displayed on practice websites for most Ontario entries (Fidler, Radovanovic, Chodos, Seidel) - only designation shown.
Quebec OPQ searches limited - no OPQ practitioner verified for this batch.
No Canadian equivalent to UK Re Y [2026] EWFC 38 - no Canadian appellate judgment formally restricting unregulated PA experts; no explicit regulatory-exclusion section.
